# Copilot / agent bootstrap

This file is the repository-wide bootstrap for any AI agent working in this
workspace. Read it before editing anything.

This repository is a **reference architecture**, not a product. Its point is the
*shape*: a governed memory layer that separates canonical records from derived
views, and keeps both honest with an executable contract. Treat the code as the
smallest faithful demonstration of that shape.

## Mandatory load order

1. Read [`governance-map.md`](governance-map.md) to learn which file is
   authoritative for a given concern.
2. Read [`instructions/governance-boundary.instructions.md`](instructions/governance-boundary.instructions.md)
   before editing any file. It encodes the four-plane model.
3. Read [`ownership-taxonomy.md`](ownership-taxonomy.md) before touching any
   Managed File (anything regenerated by automation).

## The four-plane model

| Plane | Root | Responsibility | Edit authority |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Operational | `.github/` | Loadable agent behaviour, instructions, skills | Edit with review |
| Contextual | `docs/` | Rationale, ADRs, architecture notes | Edit with review; never operational authority |
| Authority | `data/` | Canonical records, governed outputs, derived indexes | Sub-layered; see boundary instruction |
| Execution | `src/` | Runnable code, validators, CLIs | Edit with review and tests |

## Operating rules

- `docs/` may *explain* a rule and link to it. It must never *be* the rule. If a
  behaviour is required, it must already exist in `.github/`, `data/`, or `src/`.
- Records in `data/memory/` are append-only. Never rewrite history; append a new
  record or write a compacted successor file.
- The index under `data/indexes/` is **derived**. Never hand-edit it. Regenerate
  it from the canonical log.
- Any change to the record shape (`data/domains/memory-record.schema.json`) must
  update the validator in `src/governed_memory/` in the same change.
- The verifier is a gate, not advice. Do not merge a change that leaves
  `governed-memory verify` failing.

## Python environment

Use a project-local virtual environment for all Python work. Never install into
the system or user-global interpreter.
